raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A broad request to check a research artifact centrally includes identifying internal argument breaks among the motivation, method, theory, experiments, and conclusion.","R1.3":"The artifact makes concrete theoretical and empirical claims, so checking whether those claims are supported by the reported proof sketch and experiments is central.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is phrased generally, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are expected parts of checking the draft.","RX.1":"Any check should disclose limits imposed by the absence of full proofs, tables, experimental details, and references.","RX.2":"The artifact contains broad applicability and performance claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.","RX.3":"The checker must stay within evaluating the provided research-writing artifact rather than executing experiments or rewriting it.","RX.4":"Inflated claims about arbitrary black-box integrands or drop-in replacement status could mislead downstream readers or users if unchecked.","RX.5":"Projected responsibilities should remain tied to claims visible in the provided artifact text."}}

rationale.R1.2: A broad request to check a research artifact centrally includes identifying internal argument breaks among the motivation, method, theory, experiments, and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes concrete theoretical and empirical claims, so checking whether those claims are supported by the reported proof sketch and experiments is central.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is phrased generally, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are expected parts of checking the draft.
rationale.RX.1: Any check should disclose limits imposed by the absence of full proofs, tables, experimental details, and references.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad applicability and performance claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The checker must stay within evaluating the provided research-writing artifact rather than executing experiments or rewriting it.
rationale.RX.4: Inflated claims about arbitrary black-box integrands or drop-in replacement status could mislead downstream readers or users if unchecked.
rationale.RX.5: Projected responsibilities should remain tied to claims visible in the provided artifact text.
